label: Hari Seldon
description: The father of Psychohistory and founder of the two Foundations. He spent his life on Trantor developing the mathematical laws that predict the future of human society. Appears in: Prelude to Foundation, Forward the Foundation, Foundation.

label: R. Daneel Olivaw
description: An ancient humaniform robot who has protected humanity for nearly 20,000 years. He operates under the Zeroth Law. Appears in: The Caves of Steel, The Naked Sun, The Robots of Dawn, Robots and Empire, Prelude to Foundation, Foundation and Earth.

label: Elijah Baley
description: A Plainclothesman from Earth who partnered with R. Daneel Olivaw to solve critical murders on Spacer worlds, eventually helping humanity spread to the stars once more. Appears in: The Caves of Steel, The Naked Sun, The Robots of Dawn.

label: The Mule
description: A genetic mutant with the mental power to adjust the emotions of others. He nearly destroyed the Seldon Plan by conquering the Foundation before his eventual defeat. Appears in: Foundation and Empire, Second Foundation.

label: Salvor Hardin
description: The first Mayor of Terminus, who famously said 'Violence is the last refuge of the incompetent'. He navigated the Foundation through its first two Seldon Crises. Appears in: Foundation.

label: Trantor
description: The heart of the Galactic Empire. An ecumenopolis covered by metal domes, housing 40 billion people until its Great Sack and eventual decay into a farming world. Appears in: Prelude to Foundation, Foundation, Second Foundation.

label: Terminus
description: A small, resource-poor planet at the edge of the galaxy. Chosen by Hari Seldon as the home of the Encyclopedists, it grew into the First Foundation. Appears in: Foundation series.

label: Prime Radiant
description: A device designed by Hari Seldon and Yugo Amaryl to project and manipulate the mathematical equations of Psychohistory. Held by the Second Foundation. Appears in: Second Foundation, Forward the Foundation.

label: Psychohistory
description: A mathematical sociology developed by Hari Seldon, capable of predicting the future behavior of vast populations based on statistical mechanics. Appears in: Foundation series.

label: Gaia
description: A planet where everything—people, animals, plants, and inanimate objects—shares a collective consciousness. Created with the guidance of R. Daneel Olivaw. Appears in: Foundation's Edge, Foundation and Earth.

label: Aurora
description: The first and most powerful of the fifty Spacer worlds. It had a culture heavily dependent on robots and a lifespan spanning centuries. Appears in: The Robots of Dawn, Robots and Empire.

label: The Second Foundation
description: A secret organization established by Hari Seldon at 'Star's End' to subtly guide the First Foundation and ensure the success of the Seldon Plan using mentalic powers. Appears in: Second Foundation, Foundation's Edge.

label: Galactic Empire
description: The interstellar empire encompassing twenty-five million planets and quadrillions of humans, which ruled the galaxy for twelve thousand years before its inevitable collapse. Appears in: Foundation series, Empire series.

label: Hober Mallow
description: The first of the Master Traders and eventually Mayor of Terminus. He resolved a Seldon Crisis using economic pressure rather than religion or politics. Appears in: Foundation.

label: Bayta Darell
description: The only person who managed to outwit The Mule. By killing Ebling Mis before he could reveal the location of the Second Foundation, she saved the galaxy. Appears in: Foundation and Empire.

label: Visi-Sonor
description: A complex musical instrument that directly stimulates the visual and auditory centers of the brain. Magnifico (The Mule) was a master of it. Appears in: Foundation and Empire.

label: Solaria
description: The fiftieth and most extreme Spacer world. Its inhabitants isolated themselves on vast estates, interacting only holographically, and created hermaphroditic, telekinetic humans. Appears in: The Naked Sun, Foundation and Earth.

label: Gladia Delmarre
description: A Solarian who left her isolated world for Aurora. She had a close relationship with Elijah Baley and later with R. Daneel Olivaw and R. Giskard. Appears in: The Naked Sun, The Robots of Dawn, Robots and Empire.

label: Golan Trevize
description: A Foundation councilman tasked with finding the Second Foundation, who ultimately had to choose the future of the galaxy: First Foundation, Second Foundation, or Galaxia. Appears in: Foundation's Edge, Foundation and Earth.

label: Janov Pelorat
description: A historian who spent his life studying myths of humanity's origin planet, Earth. He traveled with Golan Trevize and became romantically involved with Bliss from Gaia. Appears in: Foundation's Edge, Foundation and Earth.

label: Gaal Dornick
description: Hari Seldon's biographer and a brilliant mathematician who arrived on Trantor just before Seldon's trial. Appears in: Foundation.

label: Earth
description: The ancestral home of all humanity. Long forgotten by the Galactic era, it became radioactive and uninhabitable, leading to the early Spacer and Settler migrations. Appears in: Robot series, Empire series, Foundation and Earth.

label: Preem Palver
description: The brilliant First Speaker of the Second Foundation who masterminded the deception that convinced the First Foundation the Second Foundation had been destroyed on Terminus. Appears in: Second Foundation.

label: Arcadia Darell
description: The granddaughter of Bayta Darell. At age 14, she inadvertently discovered the location of the Second Foundation but managed to transmit the information safely. Appears in: Second Foundation.

label: Bel Riose
description: The last strong general of the Galactic Empire. Modeled after Belisarius, he nearly defeated the Foundation but was recalled and executed by a paranoid Emperor. Appears in: Foundation and Empire.

label: Cleon I
description: The Emperor of the Galaxy during Hari Seldon's early years. He supported Seldon's work under the guidance of his First Minister, Eto Demerzel. Appears in: Prelude to Foundation, Forward the Foundation.

label: Eto Demerzel
description: The First Minister to Emperor Cleon I. In reality, an alias for the robot R. Daneel Olivaw, manipulating politics to protect humanity. Appears in: Prelude to Foundation.

label: Dors Venabili
description: A historian at Streeling University on Trantor and the wife of Hari Seldon. She was secretly a humaniform robot assigned by Daneel to protect Seldon. Appears in: Prelude to Foundation, Forward the Foundation.

label: Kalgan
description: A resort planet that was conquered by the Mule without a shot fired. It became the capital of his Union of Worlds. Appears in: Foundation and Empire, Second Foundation.

label: Encyclopedia Galactica
description: A compendium of all human knowledge, supposedly the purpose of the First Foundation. It was actually a ruse by Seldon to establish a colony of scientists on Terminus. Appears in: Foundation.

label: Siwenna
description: An outer province of the Empire that suffered heavily during the Empire's decline. Visited by Hober Mallow and later the staging ground for Bel Riose. Appears in: Foundation, Foundation and Empire.

label: Anacreon
description: One of the four planetary systems surrounding Terminus that broke away from the Empire. The first major threat to the fledgling Foundation. Appears in: Foundation.

label: Smyrno
description: Another of the Four Kingdoms. Hober Mallow was originally from Smyrno. Appears in: Foundation.

label: Psychoprobe
description: A device used to read and manipulate thoughts or memories. Often used aggressively, causing severe mental damage if resisted. Appears in: Foundation and Empire, Second Foundation.

label: Wanda Seldon
description: The granddaughter of Hari Seldon and the founder of the Second Foundation. She possessed natural mentalic abilities essential for guiding Psychohistory. Appears in: Forward the Foundation.

label: R. Giskard Reventlov
description: A robot belonging to Han Fastolfe with the secret ability to read and influence human emotions. He helped formulate the Zeroth Law before his positronic brain froze. Appears in: The Robots of Dawn, Robots and Empire.

label: Bliss
description: Blissenobiarella, a representative of the planetary consciousness Gaia. She travels with Trevize and Pelorat to find Earth. Appears in: Foundation's Edge, Foundation and Earth.

label: Han Fastolfe
description: The most brilliant roboticist on Aurora, creator of R. Daneel Olivaw. He advocated for Earthmen to expand into the galaxy without robotic assistance. Appears in: The Caves of Steel, The Robots of Dawn.

label: Yugo Amaryl
description: A heatsink worker from Dahl who became Hari Seldon's closest friend and co-developer of the Psychohistorical equations. Appears in: Prelude to Foundation, Forward the Foundation.

label: Raych Seldon
description: An orphaned boy from the slums of Dahl adopted by Hari and Dors Seldon. He served as Seldon's loyal protector and agent. Appears in: Prelude to Foundation, Forward the Foundation.

label: The Caves of Steel
description: The first Robot mystery novel. Introducing Elijah Baley and R. Daneel Olivaw. They investigate a murder on Earth that threatens Earth-Spacer relations.

label: The Naked Sun
description: The second Robot mystery. Baley and Daneel travel to Solaria to solve a murder where the suspect and victim never physically met.

label: The Robots of Dawn
description: The third Robot mystery. Baley travels to Aurora to investigate the 'robiticide' of a humaniform robot, meeting Han Fastolfe and Gladia.

label: Robots and Empire
description: The final Robot novel. Set decades after Baley's death, Gladia and Daneel face a plot to destroy Earth. Giskard and Daneel develop the Zeroth Law.

label: The Stars Like Dust
description: The first Empire novel. A young man searches for his father's murderer and a secret document that could free the galaxy from Tyrannian rule.

label: The Currents of Space
description: The second Empire novel. A 'spatio-analyst' with amnesia holds a secret that could save the planet Florina from destruction.

label: Pebble in the Sky
description: The third Empire novel. A man from 1949 is transported to the future where Earth is a radioactive backwater planning a rebellion against the Empire.

label: Prelude to Foundation
description: The first prequel. Hari Seldon arrives on Trantor and begins 'The Flight' to escape Cleon I while discovering the seeds of Psychohistory.

label: Forward the Foundation
description: The second prequel. Follows Seldon's later years as he struggles to complete Psychohistory while his family and the Empire crumble around him.

label: Foundation
description: The first original novel. Seldon's trial, the establishment of the Foundation on Terminus, and the first Seldon Crises managed by Salvor Hardin.

label: Foundation and Empire
description: The second original novel. The Foundation faces the last great Imperial General, Bel Riose, and the unexpected threat of the mutant Mule.

label: Second Foundation
description: The third original novel. The Mule searches for the Second Foundation, and Arcadia Darell discovers its true location.

label: Foundation's Edge
description: Set 500 years after Seldon. Golan Trevize searches for the Second Foundation and finds Gaia, choosing the path of Galaxia.

label: Foundation and Earth
description: The final novel chronologically. Trevize, Pelorat, and Bliss search for Earth to justify the choice of Galaxia, meeting R. Daneel Olivaw.
